objective: Determine whether the ideal universal primitive gate set for a dispersive transmon-cavity system survives the realistic Hamiltonian once chi, chi-prime, Kerr, and finite-duration phase accumulation are enforced, and identify the strongest physically meaningful non-GRAPE constructive alternative.
hypotheses: The strict ideal primitive gate set will not survive literally once realistic dispersive phase accumulation is enforced.

A weaker phase-aware constructive library will remain viable, built around branch-compensated displacement, spectator-limited qubit pulses, relaxed selective control, and an explicit gauge-cleanup layer.
Sequence-level runtime evidence will remain weaker than primitive-level evidence, so a fully pulse-backed non-GRAPE universal architecture will still be open.
assumptions: Inherited repository studies are the source of record for their own simulations and validations.
The nominal device point shared across the inherited studies is representative of the realistic dispersive regime of interest.
This study adds analytic synthesis and machine-readable integration rather than a new global control search.

success_criteria.analytic: Derive and visualize the timing and phase-budget conflict between unconditional and selective control.
success_criteria.integration: Consolidate the inherited primitive-level and sequence-level evidence into one machine-readable summary.
success_criteria.reporting: Produce a complete report, figures, notebook, README, and handoff files with a clear universality verdict.

completed_tasks: Audited the local cqed_sim API reference and package surface relevant to dispersive hybrid control.
Integrated the inherited displacement, waveform-level, selective-pulse, strict-SQR, relaxed-CPSQR, arbitrary-control, and runtime hybrid-unitary evidence into one synthesis dataset.
Generated machine-readable summary artifacts plus the timing-hierarchy and phase-budget figures.
Executed the reproducibility notebook end-to-end via nbconvert.
Wrote and compiled the report and bibliography.

key_results.top_level_verdict: The strict ideal primitive gate set does not survive literally once realistic dispersive dynamics are enforced. A weaker phase-aware constructive library does survive, but a fully pulse-backed non-GRAPE universal stack is not yet demonstrated.
key_results.unconditional_displacement: The best simple constructive displacement is a 20 ns two-tone branch-compensated pulse with mean broad-state fidelity 0.9857 at |chi|T/2pi = 0.057.
key_results.unconditional_qubit_rotation: A 40 ns vacuum-calibrated DRAG X_pi pulse reaches 0.99984 fidelity in vacuum but does not remain unconditional once photons occupy the cavity; the inherited study reports fidelity about 0.483 by the n = 5 manifold.
key_results.practical_selective_control: The best noisy relaxed selective rotation is a short flat-top Gaussian pulse at |chi|T/2pi = 1.0 with average relaxed fidelity 0.9903.
key_results.strict_sqr: Strict full-joint ideal SQR remains limited to easier low-dimensional direct multitone cases; the strongest inherited strict case reaches joint process fidelity 0.9988 at |chi|T/2pi = 5.0.
key_results.arbitrary_conditional_control: Hard arbitrary target classes do not support a general strict positive claim, but segmented relaxed control reaches essentially unit relaxed joint-process fidelity on difficult random-target slices.
key_results.sequence_level: The best replay-backed hybrid-unitary runtime candidate still requires GRAPE-derived local surrogates and falls to noisy average probe fidelity 0.61448 at total duration 4.432 us.

compute_notes.synthesis_build: The synthesis build reused validated inherited artifacts and completed quickly on CPU.
compute_notes.notebook: The reproducibility notebook was generated from a lightweight builder script and executed successfully via python -m jupyter nbconvert --execute.
compute_notes.report_build: The report was compiled with pdflatex, bibtex, pdflatex, pdflatex.
